description: Wave 12 FIX-WAVE - 6 defects found by an adversarial code review of the implemented Wave 12 branch (ralph/padrino-v12) that the green gates (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration' + the full --postgres suite) could NOT see. All 6 live in Wave 12's crash-recovery / concurrency / budget paths - exactly the 'operate at scale, crash-safe' properties the wave claims. Two were empirically reproduced. This fix-wave CONTINUES on the existing ralph/padrino-v12 branch (all 21 Wave 12 stories already passes:true and committed there); it does NOT branch from main. Each story applies the review's precise fix AND adds the regression test that exercises the previously-untested window (the root cause every defect shares is a test that passed because it never hit the mid-crash / co-occurrence / orphan window). Hard rules retained: pure core, hash-chain + byte-stable replay determinism, CANONICAL rating firewall, human/scientific segregation, NEVER edit shipped Alembic migrations (these fixes are code/test only - NO new migrations needed). 2 BLOCKER (US-270 resume event-log corruption + broken byte-identical replay; US-271 budget-halt + co-occurring failure corrupts campaign cell accounting), 3 SHOULD_FIX (US-272 orphaned budget-slot leak -> admission deadlock; US-273 non-atomic gauntlet/cell completion orphans a cell -> campaign never auto-finalizes; US-274 publish gate falsely blocks campaigns containing a legitimately-unbilled turn), 1 NIT (US-275 purity firewall misses os/subprocess/pathlib).

userStories.title: Crash-safe benchmark resume: truncate partial-phase events on rehydration so resume stays byte-identical
userStories.description: BLOCKER (reproduced). Benchmark resume is phase-granular: _resume_phase / _phase_started_without_resolution (game_runner.py ~765-791) resume any phase that has a persisted PhaseStarted but no PhaseResolved, and drive_game_loop then re-runs the ENTIRE phase (re-tick + re-emit every per-seat submission at game_runner.py ~1294-1312). But each submission event is persisted in its OWN transaction via _persist_stored_event (~292-298). A real process crash BETWEEN two submission commits (e.g. after 2 of 7 VoteSubmitted are durable but before PhaseResolved) leaves a PARTIAL phase. On resume rehydrate_benchmark_game (benchmark_durability.py ~33-49) replays those partial rows into the EventLog and the phase re-run appends a FRESH full set of submissions at NEW higher sequences AFTER them - no uq_game_event_sequence collision, so it ships silently - yielding DUPLICATE submission rows and a final event chain that is NOT byte-identical to the uninterrupted run (reproduced: ref_submits=7 vs resumed=9, byte_identical=False; the duplicate-bearing chain still hash-verifies, so the corruption is silent). The authoritative persisted event log is corrupted; any later replay/audit reading messages/votes sees doubled submissions. Fix: on rehydration, detect a phase that has a PhaseStarted (plus >=1 submission/resolution event) but NO PhaseResolved, and TRUNCATE those partial-phase events back to the PhaseStarted hash-chain boundary (delete the partial game_events rows and rebuild the EventLog to that head) BEFORE resuming, so the phase re-run reproduces the byte-identical tail. The US-256 byte-identical test only crashes at the PhaseStarted snapshot (before the tick), so it never covered this - strengthen it to crash AFTER a partial submission is persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: rehydrate_benchmark_game (and any shared rehydration helper) detects a partial phase - a persisted PhaseStarted with one-or-more subsequent submission/resolution events but NO PhaseResolved - and truncates those partial-phase events back to the PhaseStarted hash-chain boundary (deletes the partial game_events rows for that game and rebuilds the returned EventLog to that head) before returning the GameResume; a test asserts that after rehydration of a partial phase, the persisted game_events end exactly at PhaseStarted and the resume EventLog head hash equals the PhaseStarted event_hash.

BYTE-IDENTICAL under a MID-PHASE crash: a test runs a benchmark game uninterrupted (DeterministicMockAdapter, fixed game_seed) recording terminal_result + final event_hash + chain digest; a second run of the SAME seed is crashed AFTER N (0<N<seat_count) per-seat submissions of a phase are persisted (not at the PhaseStarted boundary), rehydrated, and resumed to terminal; the test asserts the resumed terminal_result + final event_hash + chain digest are BYTE-IDENTICAL to the uninterrupted run, and that the game_events contain exactly ONE submission per (phase, seat) - no duplicates.

The existing PhaseStarted-boundary crash case (US-256 test) still passes byte-identical (truncation is a no-op when there are no events past PhaseStarted); a test asserts the boundary-crash path is unchanged.

Pure-replay preserved: the rebuilt EventLog still passes replay_event_log hash-chain verification (a tampered row still raises); the truncation deletes only the uncommitted-phase tail, never an already-resolved phase.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: game_runner.py _resume_phase/_phase_started_without_resolution (~765-791), drive_game_loop phase re-run (~1250-1314), _persist_stored_event per-event commit (~292-298); benchmark_durability.py rehydrate (~33-49); events repo for delete-by-sequence-above. The US-256 crash hook fires via phase_snapshot with an `if not crashed` guard at tests/runner/test_benchmark_resume.py ~213 - it lands at the first PhaseStarted snapshot BEFORE the tick; the new test must inject the crash inside the per-seat submission persist loop after N submissions. Option (a) truncate-to-boundary is required (it restores byte-identical replay). NO migration. Also apply the same partial-phase truncation to the human-lane rehydration if it shares the path.

userStories.title: Budget halt must short-circuit BEFORE campaign-cell failure recording (no false dead-letter / duplicate materialization)
userStories.description: BLOCKER (reproduced). In _drive_gauntlet (runner/scheduler.py ~599-618) the order is: (1) _record_campaign_cell_failures(failures=child_result.failures) runs UNCONDITIONALLY, THEN (2) the `if child_result.budget_halts:` branch requeues the gauntlet to PENDING for a later re-run. A campaign cell materializes >=2 child games (a mirror pair) run concurrently via asyncio.gather; one game can be denied at the spend cap (_ScheduledGameBudgetHalt) while a SIBLING game transiently fails (_ScheduledGameFailure) - so both lists are non-empty. Step (1) then records the failure against the cell via record_materialized_cell_failure (campaigns.py ~383-392) BEFORE the budget requeue. With the DEFAULT padrino_campaign_cell_max_attempts=1, attempt_count 0->1 flips the cell to DEAD_LETTER even though the gauntlet was only PAUSED for budget and will be re-driven to success once budget frees - and mark_materialized_cell_completed later no-ops on the already-terminal cell, recording a PERMANENT false 'hole'. With max_attempts>=2 the failure sets the cell PENDING and clears cell.gauntlet_id=None; the budget re-run re-drives the SAME gauntlet (mark_pending re-queues the gauntlet, not the cell), and on completion _record_campaign_cell_completion can no longer find the cell (gauntlet_id nulled), so the cell stays PENDING and is independently re-materialized into a DUPLICATE gauntlet/games. The trigger (a provider blip while the spend cap is hit) is exactly the operational condition Wave 12 targets. Fix: move the budget-halt short-circuit ABOVE _record_campaign_cell_failures so a budget pause never consumes a cell retry attempt nor severs cell.gauntlet_id.
acceptanceCriteria: In _drive_gauntlet, when child_result.budget_halts is non-empty the gauntlet is requeued (gauntlets_repo.mark_pending) and the function returns BEFORE _record_campaign_cell_failures runs; a unit test asserts that a child_result with BOTH budget_halts and failures non-empty requeues the gauntlet and does NOT call record_materialized_cell_failure (the cell's attempt_count is unchanged and its status/gauntlet_id are untouched).

End-to-end (default padrino_campaign_cell_max_attempts=1): a regression test drives a campaign-cell gauntlet (>=2 child games) where ONE game is budget-denied and a SIBLING game permanently fails, then frees the budget and lets the gauntlet re-drive; the test asserts the cell is NEVER DEAD_LETTERed for the budget pause, stays MATERIALIZED+linked across the pause, and reaches COMPLETED after the budget-freed re-drive (no permanent false hole).
No duplicate materialization: the same test asserts exactly ONE gauntlet exists for the cell after the pause+re-drive (the cell was never unlinked/re-materialized into a second gauntlet).

The genuine-failure dead-letter path (a real failure with NO budget halt) is unchanged: the existing test_campaign_owned_failed_gauntlet_dead_letters_cell still passes (a cell whose games genuinely exhaust retries with no budget halt still DEAD_LETTERs).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: runner/scheduler.py _drive_gauntlet (~599-618: _record_campaign_cell_failures at ~609-614 BEFORE the budget_halts branch ~615-618); _run_child_games partitions failures vs budget_halts (~473-501); campaigns.py record_materialized_cell_failure (~383-392) and mark_materialized_cell_completed (~451-468). Default DEFAULT_CAMPAIGN_CELL_MAX_ATTEMPTS=1 (scheduler.py ~80, settings padrino_campaign_cell_max_attempts ~386). Existing budget-halt tests (~1280, ~1344) halt ALL games with no co-occurring failure; the mixed case is the untested window. NO migration.

userStories.title: Reclaim orphaned budget-reservation slots on game-lease reap + release prior slot on retry (no admission deadlock)
userStories.description: SHOULD_FIX. When a benchmark game is admitted, _run_one_game commits a live BudgetReservationSlot (global + optional per-campaign) inside session.begin() (scheduler.py ~317-331) and releases it only in the `finally` after the executor returns (~357-364). On a worker process crash / lease expiry mid-game the finally never runs, so the slot stays released_at=None FOREVER. The stale-game reaper reset_stale_games (db/repositories/games.py ~154-176) clears the game lease but does NOT release the budget slot, and nothing reads the binding_key (game:<uuid>) that was added for exactly this reclaim (binding_key is written in budget_reservations.py ~65/89 + benchmark_admission.py but has ZERO readers). Worse, when the reaped game is re-leased and retried, reserve_benchmark_game_budget claims a SECOND live slot for the same game, so the leak COMPOUNDS per retry. Cap = floor(budget/reserve) (default 200/0.5 = 400 finite slots); after ~400 crashed/retried games, claim_budget_slot returns None for every new admission even though real LlmCall spend is far below the cap - benchmark admission DEADLOCKS. It fail-closes (never overshoots) so it is a capacity leak / eventual deadlock, not an overshoot. Fix: (1) give binding_key a reader - when the reaper clears an expired game lease, release any live BudgetReservationSlot whose binding_key == game_binding_key(game_id) across BOTH the global scope and the owning campaign scope; (2) before claiming a new slot for a game, release any prior live slot(s) with the same binding_key so a reaped-then-retried game never stacks multiple live slots.
acceptanceCriteria: The stale-game reaper path (reset_stale_games or a sibling reservation reaper invoked from _reap_stale_games) releases (sets released_at) every live BudgetReservationSlot whose binding_key == game_binding_key(game_id) for each game whose expired lease it clears, across the global scope AND the owning campaign scope; a test crashes a game mid-flight (executor that abandons the slot / never returns), runs the reaper with an advanced injected clock, and asserts the live-slot count for that game's binding_key returns to 0 so admission capacity is restored.

reserve_benchmark_game_budget (or claim_budget_slot when binding_key is set) releases any prior live slot(s) with the same binding_key BEFORE claiming a new one; a test asserts a reaped-then-retried game holds at most ONE live slot per scope (the leak does not compound across retries).

No overshoot regression: the hard-ceiling behavior is unchanged - a test asserts concurrent admissions are still bounded by cap=floor(budget/reserve) and reclaimed capacity is reused by a later admission (after a crash+reap, a new game can be admitted again below cap).
The happy path is unchanged: a normally-returning game still releases its slot in the finally and live_slots returns to 0 (the existing live-slot==0 test still passes).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: scheduler.py _run_one_game slot claim (~317-331) + release-in-finally (~357-364) + _reap_stale_games (~763-775); db/repositories/games.py reset_stale_games (~154-176); economics/budget_reservations.py (binding_key write ~65/89, release_budget_slot keys on slot_id only), benchmark_admission.py (binding_key game:<uuid>), game_binding_key helper. Cap math: claim admits only when implicit_used + len(live) < cap (implicit_used = ceil(spent/reserve), summed with live - so orphaned live slots are pure additive waste). campaign_tick reserves+releases its probe slot in the same txn (binding_key campaign-tick:<id>) so it is not part of the leak. NO migration (binding_key column already exists).

userStories.title: Heal the non-atomic gauntlet-complete / cell-complete window so a crashed campaign cell can't orphan the campaign forever
userStories.description: SHOULD_FIX. _drive_gauntlet finalizes the gauntlet in its OWN session that commits internally (finalize_gauntlet_if_done does flush()+commit(), completion.py ~417-421) and only AFTERWARDS opens a SEPARATE session in _record_campaign_cell_completion (scheduler.py ~626-668) to flip the campaign cell MATERIALIZED->COMPLETED and finalize the campaign. These are two independent commits. If the process dies (or _record_campaign_cell_completion errors) AFTER the gauntlet-COMPLETED commit but BEFORE the cell-completion commit, the gauntlet is permanently COMPLETED while its cell stays MATERIALIZED. Nothing recovers it: claim_oldest_pending only claims PENDING gauntlets, the stale reapers only reset RUNNING rows, materialize_next_batch only re-materializes PENDING cells, and mark_materialized_cell_completed is invoked exclusively from this post-finalize path. MATERIALIZED is non-terminal, so campaign_progress.done never reaches total and finalize_campaign_if_done never completes the campaign - it stays RUNNING forever (the tick keeps re-leasing/heartbeating it). Fix (choose the cleaner): EITHER (A) make the gauntlet COMPLETED flip + mark_materialized_cell_completed + finalize_campaign_if_done run in a SINGLE transaction (a no-internal-commit finalize variant that accepts an open session), OR (B) add a self-healing reconciliation pass in run_campaign_tick that, before finalize_campaign_if_done, flips any of the claimed campaign's MATERIALIZED cells whose gauntlet_id resolves to a COMPLETED gauntlet to COMPLETED. Either lets finalize_campaign_if_done eventually reach done==total.
acceptanceCriteria: A crash/failure between the gauntlet-COMPLETED commit and the cell-COMPLETED commit no longer permanently orphans the cell: a test puts a campaign cell in the orphaned state (gauntlet COMPLETED, cell still MATERIALIZED) and asserts that EITHER the two commits are atomic (option A: the orphaned state is unreachable - the gauntlet flip and cell flip share one transaction, so a mid-window crash rolls back the gauntlet flip too) OR the next run_campaign_tick reconciles the MATERIALIZED-cell-with-COMPLETED-gauntlet to COMPLETED (option B).

After the fix, the campaign auto-finalizes: a test drives a campaign where one cell hits the orphan window, then runs ticks and asserts the campaign reaches COMPLETED (done==total over terminal cell statuses) - it does not stay RUNNING forever.
The happy path is unchanged: a campaign whose cells all complete normally still auto-finalizes exactly once; a test asserts no double-finalize and no duplicate cell-completion.

If option A is chosen, the no-internal-commit finalize variant does not break the cron/scheduled-gauntlet path (gauntlet_job.py finalize call, campaign-less gauntlets) - a test asserts standalone gauntlet finalization still works.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: gauntlets/completion.py finalize_gauntlet_if_done (~417-421 internal flush+commit); runner/scheduler.py _drive_gauntlet finalize vs _record_campaign_cell_completion (~626-668); db/repositories/campaigns.py mark_materialized_cell_completed (~451-468, only MATERIALIZED->COMPLETED transition), finalize_campaign_if_done (~471-490, done==total over CAMPAIGN_PAIRING_TERMINAL_STATUSES={COMPLETED,DEAD_LETTER}); campaign_tick.py (~68 claim/heartbeat, ~104 finalize). Depends on US-271 (both touch _drive_gauntlet's campaign-cell handling; build on its committed ordering). Option B (reconciliation in the tick) is the lower-blast-radius choice and self-heals existing stuck cells. NO migration.

userStories.title: Publish gate must not treat a legitimately-unbilled LLM turn as a missing cost stamp
userStories.description: SHOULD_FIX (reproduced). _cost_stamp_blockers (gauntlets/publish_gate.py ~362-396) iterates EVERY llm_call for each published (COMPLETED + terminal_result) game and emits a cost_stamp_missing blocker when cost_usd is None, and a SECOND when price_basis is None. But an unbilled turn is a NORMAL intended outcome: when an LLM call ends with status exhausted / provider_error / both_failed / primary_failed (or an empty response with no tokens), _pricing_stamp (game_runner.py ~695-704, _UNBILLED_RESULT_STATUSES ~683-685) persists the row with cost_usd=None AND price_basis=None (asserted by tests/runner/test_llm_call_cost_persistence.py ~345-379). After US-254's retry seam exhausts a transient error the engine substitutes a safe ActionTimedOut and the game still finalizes COMPLETED (tick.py ~164-191). So a perfectly valid published game routinely contains cost_usd=None / price_basis=None rows, and the gate emits two cost_stamp_missing blockers -> ready_to_publish=False PERMANENTLY (reproduced: seeding one status='exhausted' cost_usd=None price_basis=None call into the otherwise-ready world flips ready_to_publish True->False). The five shipped publish-gate tests only ever seed billed calls (cost_usd=0.01), so the gate passes CI while mis-gating real campaigns. The criterion intends to verify the IMMUTABLE stamp on BILLED calls; it conflates 'unbilled' with 'missing stamp'. Fix: skip the canonical unbilled shape (cost_usd IS None AND price_basis IS None); still flag a genuinely-broken row (billed cost_usd present but price_basis None, or price_basis set but cost_usd None).
acceptanceCriteria: _cost_stamp_blockers skips a call whose cost_usd IS None AND price_basis IS None (the canonical legitimately-unbilled shape produced by _pricing_stamp), and no longer emits cost_stamp_missing for it; a regression test seeds an 'exhausted' (cost_usd=None, price_basis=None) call into the otherwise-ready published world and asserts ready_to_publish stays True with NO cost_stamp_missing blocker.

Genuinely-broken rows are still flagged: a billed row (cost_usd not None) with price_basis None, OR a row with price_basis set but cost_usd None, still produces a cost_stamp_missing blocker; a test asserts each broken shape is still caught.
The existing price_basis validation (PRICE_BASIS_FALLBACK_TABLE requires a price_table_version, unknown basis rejected, etc.) is unchanged for billed rows; the existing publish-gate tests still pass.
A mixed game (some billed + some unbilled turns, all stamps valid on the billed ones) is publishable; a test asserts ready_to_publish is True for such a game.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: gauntlets/publish_gate.py _cost_stamp_blockers (~362-396, the two top blocker arms at ~380-396), evaluate_publish_gate ready_to_publish = not blockers (~183); game_runner.py _pricing_stamp (~695-704) + _UNBILLED_RESULT_STATUSES (~683-685); db/repositories/llm_calls.record_call inserts every result (no status filter); tick.py exhausted->ActionTimedOut (~164-191); tests/runner/test_llm_call_cost_persistence.py (~345-379 asserts the unbilled None/None shape). The five publish-gate tests seed only billed cost_usd=0.01 rows - none seed the unbilled shape. NO migration.

userStories.title: Close the pure-core purity firewall gap: forbid os / subprocess / pathlib top-level imports in core
userStories.description: NIT (weakened test, not a live bug - shipped core is clean). The repo-wide pure-core firewall tests/core/test_purity.py rglob-scans every core module and forbids a FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL set (random, secrets, asyncio, datetime, time, sqlalchemy, litellm, httpx + padrino.{db,llm,api,runner}) - but it OMITS os, subprocess, and pathlib. os.urandom() is exactly the unseeded-randomness escape hatch SeededRng exists to prevent and os.environ is env/secret access - both within the AGENTS.md pure-core ban - yet `import os` (or subprocess/pathlib) in any core module passes both test_purity.py and the pairing-specific tests/core/test_pairing.py purity guard (verified: injecting `import os` into core leaves both GREEN; injecting `import asyncio` correctly fails test_purity.py - so asyncio is already covered, only os/subprocess/pathlib leak). tests/core/test_no_wallclock.py only matches call-expressions (datetime.now()/time.time()), never imports, so it does not backstop the gap. Fix: add os, subprocess, pathlib to FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL in tests/core/test_purity.py (closes the gap for EVERY core module at once); optionally add os to the pairing-specific denylist as belt-and-suspenders.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/core/test_purity.py FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL includes os, subprocess, and pathlib (in addition to the existing entries); a test (or a comment-documented manual check) confirms that injecting `import os` into any core module makes test_purity.py go RED.

The shipped core is still clean: test_purity.py PASSES on the unmodified branch (no core module currently imports os/subprocess/pathlib at top level) - the fix is a stricter guard, not a code change to core.

The pairing-specific purity guard (tests/core/test_pairing.py) optionally also denylists os for belt-and-suspenders, but the load-bearing fix is the repo-wide FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL; do NOT add asyncio anywhere new (test_purity.py already catches it).
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: tests/core/test_purity.py FORBIDDEN_TOP_LEVEL (~21-39, rglob-scans all core modules); tests/core/test_pairing.py purity guard (~171-181, denylist lacks os); tests/core/test_no_wallclock.py (~41-49, call-expression only). Verified: `import os` passes both today; `import asyncio` already fails test_purity.py. Test-only change, NO source change to core, NO migration.
